data.body: Step 2-3 Investigation (inline fallback): ROOT_CAUSE confirmed. The test test_cross_stack_multi_marker_python_priority referenced a wrong fixture path: tests/evals/fixtures/multi-marker-project instead of tests/fixtures/multi-marker-project. The path does not exist, so the guard branch assert_eq fires 'exists' vs 'missing' causing a FAILED assertion. Additionally, the fixture tests/fixtures/multi-marker-project was not yet created. Both issues were fixed in commit 1f5e79616d (2026-04-25) which: (1) corrected the path reference in the test, (2) created the fixture directory with pyproject.toml and package.json. Hypothesis tested empirically: tests/evals/fixtures/multi-marker-project does not exist (confirmed), tests/fixtures/multi-marker-project exists (confirmed). Test now passes 3/3 consecutive runs. RESOLUTION: already fixed by commit 1f5e79616d.
